introSection: Joker's Jinx was a steel launched roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened in 1999, the ride uses linear induction motor (LIM) technology to launch riders from to  in 3 seconds, without touching the bottom of the train.

sections.title: History
sections.content:
Premier Rides track pieces were spotted on the property in the fall of 1998. On October 28, 1998, it was announced that Adventure World would be renamed Six Flags America and feature several new attractions, including Joker's Jinx and Two-Face: The Flip Side.

Construction was completed in March 1999. Testing then began on April 4, 1999.

On May 8, 1999, both Joker's Jinx and Two-Face: The Flip Side opened.

When the ride first opened, it had over-the-shoulder restraints, which caused a rough ride. As a result, riders who returned back into the station had either bleeding earlobes or were dizzy from the side-to-side pounding of their heads against the restraints. In order to allow a smoother ride, the over-the-shoulder restraints were replaced with lap bars in 2002.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to Joker's Jinx.

Joker's Jinx closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

sections.title: Design
sections.content: Joker's Jinx begins with a launch that sends riders through a tunnel into a "spaghetti bowl" section of track which leads into a cobra roll. Next, the ride travels through a sidewinder followed by several winding curves and a corkscrew.

sections.title: Color scheme
sections.content: Joker's Jinx has a green track and purple supports.

sections.title: Incidents
sections.content: On August 11, 2014, a train full of riders stalled near the top of the ride. It took five hours for the fire brigade to evacuate all riders. The ride remained closed while an investigation was completed.

fullText:
Joker's Jinx was a steel launched roller coaster formerly located at Six Flags America in Upper Marlboro, Maryland, USA. Opened in 1999, the ride uses linear induction motor (LIM) technology to launch riders from to  in 3 seconds, without touching the bottom of the train.

Premier Rides track pieces were spotted on the property in the fall of 1998. On October 28, 1998, it was announced that Adventure World would be renamed Six Flags America and feature several new attractions, including Joker's Jinx and Two-Face: The Flip Side.

Construction was completed in March 1999. Testing then began on April 4, 1999.

On May 8, 1999, both Joker's Jinx and Two-Face: The Flip Side opened.

When the ride first opened, it had over-the-shoulder restraints, which caused a rough ride. As a result, riders who returned back into the station had either bleeding earlobes or were dizzy from the side-to-side pounding of their heads against the restraints. In order to allow a smoother ride, the over-the-shoulder restraints were replaced with lap bars in 2002.

In 2022, a single rider line was added to Joker's Jinx.

Joker's Jinx closed permanently on November 2, 2025, along with Six Flags America.

Joker's Jinx begins with a launch that sends riders through a tunnel into a "spaghetti bowl" section of track which leads into a cobra roll. Next, the ride travels through a sidewinder followed by several winding curves and a corkscrew.

Joker's Jinx has a green track and purple supports.



On August 11, 2014, a train full of riders stalled near the top of the ride. It took five hours for the fire brigade to evacuate all riders. The ride remained closed while an investigation was completed.
